q: The SI unit of time is:
sol: Second is the international standard unit for measuring time.

q: A simple pendulum takes 32s to complete 20 oscillations. What is the time period?
sol: Time period = Total time / Number of oscillations = 32 / 20 = 1.6 s.

q: The distance-time graph for an object moving with a constant speed is a:
sol: A straight line indicates that distance increases linearly with time (constant speed).

q: Which device is used to measure the distance moved by a vehicle?
sol: Odometer records the total distance traveled by the vehicle.

q: Speed is equal to:
sol: Speed is defined as the rate of change of distance per unit time.

q: The basic unit of speed is:
sol: Meters per second (m/s) is the standard SI unit for speed.

q: An object moving along a straight line with a constant speed is said to be in:
sol: Uniform motion occurs when an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time.

q: Which of the following cannot be used for the measurement of time?
o: Shadow of an object during the day
sol: Blinking is not a periodic event with a fixed time interval.

q: The time taken by a pendulum to complete one oscillation is called its:
sol: Time period is the specific duration for one full back-and-forth motion.

q: Which of these is a periodic motion?
o: A car moving on a straight road
A stone falling from a height
Motion of the earth around the sun
sol: Earth's orbit repeats at regular intervals (approx. 365 days).

q: If a car travels 60 km in 2 hours, its speed is:
sol: Speed = 60 / 2 = 30 km/h.

q: Sundials were used in ancient times to measure time based on:
sol: Sundials use the position and length of shadows cast by the sun.

q: Quartz clocks are more accurate than ancient clocks because they use:
sol: Vibrations of quartz crystals provide a very steady and precise frequency.

q: The fastness or slowness of an object is decided by its:
sol: Speed tells us how much distance is covered in a certain time.

q: If the distance-time graph is a horizontal line parallel to the time axis, the object is:
sol: A flat line means distance is not changing as time passes, so the object is stationary.

q: The motion of a swing is an example of:
sol: A swing repeats its path in regular intervals of time.

q: How many seconds are there in one hour?
sol: 1 hour = 60 minutes and 1 minute = 60 seconds; 60 × 60 = 3600.

q: Which of the following is not a unit of time?
sol: A light year is a unit of distance, not time.

q: A cheetah can run at a speed of 100 km/h. How much distance will it cover in 15 minutes?
sol: 15 min = 0.25 h; Distance = 100 × 0.25 = 25 km.

q: In a simple pendulum, the metallic ball is called the:
sol: The small heavy mass suspended is known as the bob.

q: Slope of a distance-time graph represents:
sol: The steeper the slope, the higher the speed of the object.

q: Which instrument shows the speed of a vehicle in km/h?
sol: Speedometer displays the instantaneous speed of the vehicle.

q: Non-uniform motion is characterized by:
sol: In non-uniform motion, the speed varies during the travel.

q: The movement of a clock's hands is:
sol: The hands repeat their motion every hour, minute, or 12 hours.

q: Galileo Galilei is famous for his work on:
sol: Galileo discovered that a pendulum of a given length takes the same time for each oscillation.
